mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window or forward returns show no statistical significance during rate cuts.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.5 percentage points signals deteriorating household income and reduced consumer spending, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in > 55% of triggered months or the signal loses predictive power when unemployment rises during recessionary periods.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates robust aggregate demand, which sustains corporate earnings growth and produces positive quarterly S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a single-series baseline, or quarterly returns are negative in > 60% of co-expansion periods.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month rebound in the index of industrial production above 1.0% signals supply chain normalization and capacity utilization recovery, triggering short-term positive equity momentum.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in > 50% of rebound months or the signal reverses direction during supply-constrained inflationary periods.

mechanism_narrative: Low federal funds rates combined with rapidly rising unemployment indicate accommodative policy failing to offset labor market deterioration, creating negative equity returns as recession fears dominate.
failure_condition: The interaction term shows no incremental predictive power over main effects, or forward returns turn positive when both conditions are met.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth exceeding 2.0% annualized confirms strong macroeconomic momentum, which translates into higher corporate profit margins and positive quarterly S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: Quarterly returns are negative in > 55% of high-growth quarters or the threshold fails to separate return distributions significantly.

mechanism_narrative: Declines in the industrial production index reflect weakening manufacturing activity and reduced capital expenditure expectations, generating negative short-term S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns show no negative correlation with INDPRO declines or Rank IC remains below 0.01 over rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment growth exceeding 200,000 per month during periods of stable inflation signals healthy labor demand, supporting consumer spending and driving positive monthly equity returns.
failure_condition: The signal loses predictive accuracy when inflation accelerates, or forward returns are negative in > 50% of triggered months.
